fundamentals.name: Starbucks Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Starbucks Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, operates as a roaster, marketer, and retailer of coffee internationally. The company operates through three segments: North America, International, and Channel Development. Its stores offer coffee, tea, and other beverages, roasted whole beans and ground coffees, complementary food, packaged coffees, single-serve products, and ready-to-drink beverages; and various food products, such as pastries, breakfast sandwiches, and lunch items. The company also licenses its trademarks through licensed stores, and grocery and foodservice accounts. The company offers its products under the Starbucks Coffee, Teavana, Seattle's Best Coffee, Ethos, and Starbucks Reserve brands. Starbucks Corporation was founded in 1971 and is based in Seattle, Washington.

judgment.summary: A company with strong earnings growth and a decent analyst consensus, but facing headwinds from high valuation, negative free cash flow, and a high debt load.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 14.5%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Earnings growth of 32.6% YoY and quarterly earnings growth of 33.0% indicate strong profitability.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $106.58, suggesting upside potential.
Dividend yield of 2.5% offers a modest return to shareholders.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 77.76 and Forward P/E of 33.66 indicate a high valuation.
Free cash flow is negative at $-1.30B TTM, raising concerns about cash generation.
Total debt of $24.41B is substantial relative to total cash of $1.70B.
Dividend payout ratio of 187.8% is unsustainable long-term.

news.paragraph: Starbucks is reportedly ending its AI inventory program across North American stores due to inaccuracies, a move that follows a blunt message from the CEO regarding consumer spending. While one analyst report highlights Starbucks as a 'Bull of the Day,' the decision to pull back on AI initiatives suggests potential execution challenges or a reassessment of technology investments. The company's next earnings date is July 28th.

headlines.title: Reuters: Starbucks Ends AI Inventory Program across North American Stores
Starbucks CEO sends blunt message on consumer spending
Arm shares extend weekly rally to almost 50%, and Starbucks pulls the plug on an AI project
